violations.text: Raw animal foods (liquid eggs) were observed stored above ready-to-eat foods (drinks) in the walk-in refrigerator. Raw animal foods must be separated from raw ready-to-eat food such as vegetables during storage. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/09/2026

The door to the outside, located at the back door, is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New Violation. Correct By: 02/23/2026

Wood is used in the construction of prep area ledge. Non-food contact surfaces of equipment that are exposed to splash, spillage, or other food soiling or that require frequent cleaning must be constructed of a nonabsorbent and smooth material. New Violation. Correct By: 02/23/2026

The chlorine sanitizer concentration for the sanitizing cycle of the dishwasher was 0 parts per million (ppm). Chlorine sanitizing solutions must be between 50-100 ppm. Dishwasher was primed to 50 ppm during time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/10/2025

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (can opener) were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/10/2025

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (ice machine) were observed with an accumulation of biofilm. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Ice was voluntarily disposed of (see attached disposal form). Ice machine was taken out of service and must not be used until is cleaned. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/10/2025

Food was observed not marked with the date of preparation. Ready-to-eat time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods prepared in the establishment and held for more than 24 hours must be marked with the date of preparation. New Violation. Correct By: 02/10/2025

The sink used for food preparation is lacking an air gap. A direct connection must not exist between the sewage system and a drain originating from equipment in which food is placed. New Violation. Correct By: 02/24/2025

The dishwasher failed to wash his/her hands between loading soiled and unloading clean dishes from the dishwasher. Food employees must clean their hands after handling soiled equipment or utensils. New Violation. Correct By: 09/06/2023

Soap was not available at the hand-washing sink in the kitchen ware-washing area. Hand-washing sinks must be provided with a supply of hand cleaning liquid, powder, or bar soap. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 09/20/2023

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (ice machine shield) were observed with an accumulation of biofilm. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. The ice was voluntarily disposed of during time of inspection (see attached disposal form). Ice machine must be cleaned. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 09/20/2023

Food was observed held at 44-50 degrees Fahrenheit in the pantry refrigerator. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Per person in charge, food had not been out of temperature for more than 4 hours and a temperature log was provided. All time/temperature control for safety foods were relocated to the refrigeration unit in the kitchen. The ambient air temperature was observed at 43-45 degrees Fahrenheit during time of inspection. Establishment must not use the pantry refrigeration unit for storing time/temperature control for safety foods until it can maintain an internal food temperature of 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below. Refrigerator must be repaired/replaced/adjusted. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 09/20/2023
